🔍 DeepFake Detection Report

EfficientNet-B4 + MTCNN + Grad-CAM Explainability

Generated on February 25, 2026 — 22:01

🏗️ Model Architecture

ComponentDetails
BackboneEfficientNet-B4 (ImageNet Pretrained)
Input Resolution380 × 380 px
Face DetectorMTCNN (Multi-task CNN)
Classifier2-class (REAL / FAKE)
Dropout0.5
ExplainabilityGrad-CAM (last conv layer)

⚙️ Training Configuration

ParameterValue
DatasetFaceForensics++ (C23 Compression)
Data Sampling12% per folder (~840 videos, ~44K face images)
OptimizerAdamW (weight_decay=1e-4)
Learning Rate0.001 (backbone: 0.0001 after unfreeze)
LR ScheduleCosineAnnealingWarmRestarts (T₀=5, T_mult=2)
Batch Size8
Mixed PrecisionAMP (FP16) — 2x speedup
Class BalancingWeightedRandomSampler
AugmentationFlip, Rotation, ColorJitter, GaussianBlur, RandomErasing, Grayscale
Early StoppingPatience = 5 epochs
Training StrategyPhase 1: Frozen backbone (2 epochs) → Phase 2: Full fine-tuning

📊 Test Performance

99.1%
Accuracy
99.95%
Precision
98.96%
Recall
99.45%
F1 Score

Evaluated on 6597 unseen test images (Real: 908 | Fake: 5689)

🧮 Confusion Matrix

MetricCountInterpretation
True Positives (TP)5630Correctly detected fakes
True Negatives (TN)905Correctly identified real faces
False Positives (FP)3Real faces wrongly flagged as fake
False Negatives (FN)59Fake faces that slipped through

🧠 Grad-CAM Explainability Analysis

Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) generates heatmaps showing which facial regions the model focuses on to make its prediction. Red/yellow areas indicate high attention, blue areas indicate low attention.

✅ Real Face Samples

For real faces, the model typically focuses on natural skin texture consistency, particularly around the nose and cheek areas — regions where authentic skin patterns are most pronounced.

✅ REAL — 100.0% confidence
Source: original/996
✅ REAL — 100.0% confidence
Source: original/388
✅ REAL — 100.0% confidence
Source: original/336

🚨 Fake Face Samples

For deepfake faces, the model highlights face-swap boundaries — eyes, jawline, mouth edges, and nose bridge. These are regions where deepfake synthesis algorithms produce visible blending artifacts.

🚨 FAKE — 99.8% confidence
Source: Deepfakes/887_275
🚨 FAKE — 100.0% confidence
Source: Deepfakes/593_577
🚨 FAKE — 100.0% confidence
Source: Deepfakes/722_458
🚨 FAKE — 100.0% confidence
Source: Face2Face/696_686

🔑 Key Findings

#Finding
1EfficientNet-B4 with ImageNet pretraining achieves 99.1% accuracy with only 12% data sampling
22-phase training (frozen → unfrozen backbone) enables rapid convergence in ~7 epochs
3Only 3 false positives — when the model says "FAKE", it's correct 99.95% of the time
4Grad-CAM confirms the model detects genuine deepfake artifacts at face-swap boundaries, not spurious correlations
5WeightedRandomSampler effectively handles 1:6 real-to-fake class imbalance
🔍 DeepFake Detection Report — EfficientNet-B4 + MTCNN + Grad-CAM
